This genus is further classified under the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes many species of grasses. intro: Echinochloa najada, also known as Japanese millet or Echinochloa millet, is a type of annual grass native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Echinochloa najada include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Echinochloa najada is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or dried powder, and is also used in the form of granules or capsules for various health conditions.
